trivia_bullets.text: Blossoming Sands is part of the ten-card 'gain land' cycle that debuted in Khans of Tarkir (2014), covering all five allied and five enemy color pairs. The cycle was a functional expansion of the earlier five-card 'Refuge' cycle from Zendikar (2009), which only covered allied color pairs. Khans was the first set to complete the full ten-land gain-land suite. [MTG Wiki: Tapland, via WebSearch snippet]

The M21 printing carries the original Khans of Tarkir artwork by Sam Burley (illustration ID 0e1ebbaa), the same illustration used for the Commander 2017 reprint. Burley served as Magic's in-house concept artist at Wizards of the Coast for roughly five years before leaving to freelance in 2018; over half of his roughly 80 Magic cards are basic or nonbasic lands. [Hipsters of the Coast: 'Sam Burley Talks About His Theros Beyond Death Lands', 2020]
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Sam Burley THB Lands article (2020)

trivia_bullets.text: Blossoming Sands is legal in Pauper and Pauper Commander, where gain lands function as the primary budget dual-land option for two-color mana fixing. The 1-life bonus partially offsets the tempo loss from entering tapped, making the cycle more durable in attrition-heavy formats than simple taplands that gain nothing. EDHREC rank: 1059 (common), indicating broad Commander adoption. [Scryfall legalities; EDHREC rank via Scryfall API]

As of Tarkir: Dragonstorm (2025), Blossoming Sands has been printed 17 times across sets including Commander precons, Masters sets (Eternal Masters, 2016), Ikoria, Kamigawa: Neon Dynasty, March of the Machine, Bloomburrow, Foundations, and Aetherdrift. Its near-constant reprint cadence reflects Wizards' policy of seeding cheap mana-fixing into every format's accessible card pool. [Scryfall prints search]
